title: iac-support.json
description: Phase 0 Assess Step 2b output: IaC provider discovery. Records which IaC formats the service is reachable from (CloudFormation, Terraform, CDK, CDKTF) plus any user-requested format overrides. Produced by skills/assess. Consumed by skills/generate-iac to decide which format subdirectories to populate. If the file is missing, generate-iac defaults to CloudFormation only (see assess/SKILL.md Step 2b).

discovered_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp marking when the IaC discovery ran.

resource_types.description: Every entry must match the AWS::<CfnPrefix>::<Type> pattern.

provider.description: Which HashiCorp Terraform AWS provider covers the service. 'awscc' uses the Cloud Control-backed provider; 'aws' uses the native HashiCorp AWS provider; 'both' when native and awscc each cover distinct resource subsets; 'none' when no Terraform path exists yet.

construct_level.description: L1 = CloudFormation-direct (Cfn* constructs), L2 = high-level constructs, L2-alpha = experimental L2, none = no CDK module.

derives_from.description: Which Terraform provider CDKTF bindings derive from. Mirrors terraform.provider values.

generate_formats.description: The final decision matrix consumed by skills/generate-iac. A 'true' entry means Phase 4 will populate that format's subdirectory; 'false' means skip. Values must be the logical AND of the corresponding <format>.supported flag and any user_override NOT skipping that format.

action.description: 'skip' excludes a supported format; 'force' is not allowed when the format is unsupported (see assess/SKILL.md Step 2b: "If a format is NOT supported, it cannot be forced").

warnings.description: Free-text warnings surfaced during IaC discovery (e.g., 'Terraform aws provider coverage partial — falling back to awscc for 3 resource types').
